e: 'Manifiesto' (manifesto) is a noun meaning a public declaration of intentions, motives, or views. Example: 'Publicaron el manifesto este verano' (They published the manifesto this summer).
'Colina' (hill) is a feminine noun meaning a natural elevation of land, smaller than a mountain. Example: 'El jeep trepaba la colina sin dificultad' (The jeep climbed the hill without difficulty).
'Descendiente' (descendant) is a noun referring to a person descended from an ancestor. Example: 'Es una típica descendiente de árabes' (She is a typical Arab descendant).

'Manta' (blanket) is a feminine noun meaning a large piece of thick woven material used to keep warm. Example: 'Les enrollaba la manta alrededor del cuello' (She would wrap the blanket around their necks).

'Realista' (realistic) is an adjective meaning representing things accurately or in a practical manner. Example: 'Me gusta mucho el arte realista del siglo 19' (I really like the realistic art of the 19th century).
'Guante' (glove) is a masculine noun meaning a covering for the hand with separate sections for each finger. Example: 'Siempre lleva guantes en el invierno' (She always wears gloves in the winter).

'Petrolero' (oil/petroleum) is an adjective relating to oil or petroleum. Example: 'Se realizan exploración petrolera en comunidades amazónicas' (Oil exploration is being carried out in Amazon communities).

'Show' (show/display) is a noun borrowed from English, referring to a television show or public performance. Example: 'La estrella del show televisivo empezará su carrera musical' (The superstar from the TV show will begin her musical career).
'Opositor' (opponent) is a noun meaning someone who opposes or competes against. Example: 'Los opositores al Presidente gritan rabiosos' (The president's opponents yell furiously).

'Marítimo' (maritime/marine) is an adjective relating to the sea or ocean. Example: 'El comercio marítimo internacional equivale a 1.500 metros cúbicos' (International maritime trade equals 1,500 cubic meters).
'Raya' (line/stripe) is a feminine noun meaning a thin mark or line. Example: 'Esculpió una raya en la madera' (He carved a line in the wood).

'Preservar' (to preserve) is a verb meaning to maintain or protect something from decay or change. Example: 'Hay que preservar el español de los antepasados' (You have to preserve the Spanish of your ancestors).

'Cañón' (canyon/cannon) is a masculine noun with two meanings: a deep valley with steep sides, or an artillery weapon. Example: 'Mi hermano se casó en el cañón de roca roja en Nevada' (My brother married in Red Rock Canyon in Nevada).

'Capricho' (caprice/whim) is a masculine noun meaning a sudden, unpredictable desire or change of mood. Example: 'No creáis que lo hacemos por mero capricho' (Don't believe we do this for a mere whim).
'Nido' (nest) is a masculine noun meaning a structure built by birds for laying eggs. Example: 'El cuco cría a sus polluelos en nido ajeno' (The cuckoo raises its young in the nest of others).
'Almorzar' (to have lunch) is a verb meaning to eat the midday meal. Example: 'Era la hora de almorzar una comida ligera' (It was time to have a light lunch).

'Murmullo' (murmur/whispering) is a masculine noun meaning a soft, continuous sound like whispering. Example: 'Solo se ha escuchado una pequeña protesta, casi un murmullo' (There has only been a small protest, almost a murmur).

'Terremoto' (earthquake) is a masculine noun meaning a violent shaking of the earth's surface. Example: 'Todo vibra porque un terremoto es muy fuerte' (Everything shakes because an earthquake is very strong).
'Sereno' (serene/calm) is an adjective meaning peaceful and untroubled. Example: 'Ya está muy sereno tras la tormenta' (It is already very calm after the storm).

'Monarquía' (monarchy) is a feminine noun meaning a form of government headed by a monarch. Example: 'Los británicos llegaron a ser una monarquía constitucional' (The British became a constitutional monarchy).

'Analista' (analyst) is a noun referring to someone who analyzes data, information, or events. Example: 'Muchos analistas creen que es una guerra entre Irán y sus rivales árabes' (Many analysts believe it's a war between Iran and their Arab rivals).

'Carreta' (cart/wagon) is a feminine noun meaning a vehicle pulled by animals, typically used for cargo. Example: 'La carreta dejará de moverse porque ha cesado la fuerza de los bueyes' (The cart will stop moving because the oxen have stopped pulling it).

'Retiro' (withdrawal/retreat) is a masculine noun meaning the act of withdrawing or removing oneself. Example: 'El retiro espiritual me ayudará a recuperar' (The spiritual retreat will help me recover).

'Disputa' (dispute/argument) is a feminine noun meaning a disagreement or argument between parties. Example: 'Refleja tensiones por la disputa de la candidatura presidencial' (It reflects tensions over the disputed presidential candidacy).

'Costarricense' (Costa Rican) is an adjective referring to people or things from Costa Rica. Example: 'Querían cambiar la vida urbana del pueblo costarricense' (They wanted to change urban life for the Costa Rican people).

'Consuelo' (comfort/consolation) is a masculine noun meaning solace or comfort in times of distress. Example: 'Le murmuraba que hay un consuelo para cada dolor' (He murmured that there is relief for every pain).

'Graduar' (to adjust/graduate) is a verb with two meanings: to graduate from school or to adjust something in degrees. Example: 'Los estudiantes que aspiran a graduarse de bachilleres se esfuerzan más' (Students who intend to graduate from high school work harder).
'Olímpico' (Olympic/Olympian) is an adjective relating to the Olympic Games. Example: 'El popular boxeador chino, doble campeón Olímpico' (The popular Chinese boxer, two-time Olympic champion).

'Química' (chemistry) is a feminine noun referring to the science that deals with substances and their reactions. Example: 'Los conceptos que pertenecen a la física, a la química y a la mecánica' (Concepts that pertain to physics, chemistry, and mechanics).

'Sugerencia' (suggestion) is a feminine noun meaning a proposal or idea offered for consideration. Example: 'Habíamos recibido la sugerencia, el deseo, incluso la petición directa' (We had received the suggestion, the wish, including the direct request).

'Dinámico' (dynamic) is an adjective meaning characterized by energy or effective action. Example: 'Un mundo efervescente, dinámico, rico en posibilidades' (An effervescent, dynamic world, full of possibilities).

s: 'verter' means 'to pour, spill, shed' in Spanish
e: 'Verter' (to pour/spill) is a verb meaning to cause liquid to flow or to shed. Example: 'El devota vierte agua sobre la imagen de Buda' (The devotee pours water over the Buddha statue).
'Frecuentemente' (frequently/often) is an adverb meaning happening often or at regular intervals. Example: 'Suelo pensar frecuentemente en mi familia' (I usually think frequently about my family).

'Interrogar' (to question/interrogate) is a verb meaning to ask questions, especially in a formal or intense manner. Example: 'Comienzo a interrogar a todos los criminales' (I am beginning to question all the criminals).

s: 'Irregular' is an adjective meaning not regular, not consistent, or not following a pattern. Example: 'Fue por un recorrido irregular, con curvas periódicas' (It went by an irregular path, with recurring curves).
q: What Spanish word is related to "regular"?
e: 'Irregular' (irregular) is an adjective meaning not regular, not consistent, or not following a pattern. Example: 'Fue por un recorrido irregular, con curvas periódicas' (It went by an irregular path, with recurring curves).

s: 'rematar' means 'to finish off, finish up' in Spanish
e: 'Rematar' (to finish off/finish up) is a verb meaning to complete or conclude something, often with a final or decisive action. Example: 'Era el momento de rematar el cuento' (It was the moment to finish up the story).

'Creatividad' (creativity) is a feminine noun meaning the ability to create or produce original ideas. Example: 'Celebrando la creatividad e innovación en los medios latinoamericanos' (Celebrating the creativity and innovations of Latin American media).

'Oveja' (sheep/ewe) is a feminine noun referring to the woolly mammal. Example: 'Se ganó el título de «oveja negra» durante la adolescencia' (He earned the title of 'black sheep' when he was an adolescent).

'Advertencia' (warning/notice) is a feminine noun meaning a notification to be aware of danger or importance. Example: 'En tono de advertencia dijo: tenga cuidado' (In a warning tone he said: be careful).
'Anular' (to annul/cancel) is a verb meaning to nullify or cancel something that was previously valid. Example: 'Anularon su vacación en el Caribe' (They canceled their vacation in the Caribbean).

'Manipular' (to manipulate/handle) is a verb meaning to handle or operate something with the hands, or to influence something. Example: 'Empezó a manipular la cerradura de la puerta' (He started to manipulate the door lock).

'Operador' (operator) is a masculine noun referring to someone who operates machinery or equipment. Example: 'No se trata sólo de un tema de operadores o usuarios' (It is not only about the issue of operators or users).

'Restaurar' (to restore/reinstate) is a verb meaning to bring something back to its original condition or position. Example: 'Intentaban restaurar a Bonaparte en el trono' (They were trying to restore Bonaparte to the throne).
'Diariamente' (daily) is an adverb meaning every day or on a daily basis. Example: 'Corre diariamente con su perro en el parque' (He runs daily with his dog in the park).
'Vibrar' (to vibrate/tremble) is a verb meaning to move back and forth rapidly. Example: 'La música hacía vibrar las ventanas de vidrio' (The music made the glass windows vibrate).
'Coser' (to sew) is a verb meaning to join fabric or materials using needle and thread. Example: 'Veía a su madre coser los vestuarios' (He watched his mother sew costumes).

'Implementar' (to implement) is a verb meaning to put a plan or system into action. Example: 'Explicó cómo implementar un programa de innovación en su empresa' (He explained how to set up an innovative program for his company).
'Suicidio' (suicide) is a masculine noun referring to the act of intentionally ending one's own life. Example: 'Fracasó en su intento de suicidio' (He failed in his attempt to commit suicide).
"sistemático" is a Spanish adjective that means "systematic". Example: "era sistemático e incluso resultaba monótono" (it was systematic and even monotonous)

"valoración" is a Spanish noun that means "assessment, valuation, evaluation". Example: "Ya se habrá dado cuenta de la valoración (positiva o negativa)." (He has already figured out the assessment (positive or negative).)
"florecer" is a Spanish verb that means "to flower, bloom, flourish". Example: "ahorita van a florecer las petunias" (the petunias are going to bloom now)
"ascenso" is a Spanish noun that means "promotion, climb". Example: "fue un ascenso en mi carrera, y era lo que yo quería" (it was a promotion in my career, and it was what I wanted)
"pánico" is a Spanish noun that means "panic". Example: "con pánico lo busqué, por supuesto, ¡no estaba!" (I looked for it in a panic, of course, it wasn’t there!)
"talla" is a Spanish noun that means "size (of clothing), stature". Example: "entre la talla cuarenta y dos y la cuarenta y cuatro" (between size forty-two and forty-four)
"acta" is a Spanish noun that means "(meeting) proceedings, minutes". Example: "parecían actas de reuniones del Partido político" (it looked like the minutes of the meeting of the political party)
"inclinación" is a Spanish noun that means "slope, incline, tendency". Example: "me da miedo la inclinación de las carreteras en los Andes" (the slope of the roads in the Andes frightens me)
"veinticuatro" is a Spanish noun that means "twenty-four". Example: "se quedan abiertas las veinticuatro horas" (they stay open twenty-four hours)
"deducir" is a Spanish verb that means "to deduce, infer". Example: "no debe ser tan complicado deducir cuánto cuesta eso" (it shouldn’t be that complicated to figure out how much that costs)

"nene" is a Spanish noun that means "baby, small child". Example: "no puedo evitar que un nene de 11 meses se lo lleve todo a la boca" (I can’t stop a baby of 11 months from putting everything in his mouth)

"reja" is a Spanish noun that means "grill, railing". Example: "esos prisioneros siempre serán libres aunque estén detrás de las rejas" (those prisoners will always be free even if they are behind bars)

s: "Ingrediente" is a Spanish noun that refers to items used in cooking or preparation. Example: "pan de papas: Ingredientes: 10 papas, limpias, lavadas . . ." (potato bread: ingredients: 10 potatoes (clean and washed) . . .)
q: What Spanish word is related to "week"?
e: "ingrediente" is a Spanish noun that means "ingredient". Example: "pan de papas: Ingredientes: 10 papas, limpias, lavadas . . ." (potato bread: ingredients: 10 potatoes (clean and washed) . . .)
"hectárea" is a Spanish noun that means "hectare". Example: "una extensión de doscientas hectáreas en el centro de un valle" (an area of two hundred hectares in the center of a valley)

"prestación" is a Spanish noun that means "benefit, lending of service". Example: "los fabricantes mejoran su rentabilidad por la prestación de servicios" (the manufacturers are increasing their profits by providing professional services)

"hormona" is a Spanish noun that means "hormone". Example: "procesos metabólicos se regulan por hormonas del sistema endócrino" (metabloic processes are regulated by hormones from the endocrine system)
"centavo" is a Spanish noun that means "cent". Example: "con un poquito de crema que le costaba cinco centavos" (with a little bit of cream that would cost him five cents)

"idear" is a Spanish verb that means "to devise, design". Example: "los grandes genios de la historia logran idear alguna cosa novedosa" (the great geniuses of history manage to think up something new)

"abismo" is a Spanish noun that means "abyss, large gap". Example: "empiezan a cerrar el abismo que separa a los países ricos de los pobres" (they are beginning to close the gap that separates the rich and poor countries)
"sanguíneo" is a Spanish adjective that means "blood, bloody". Example: "Los ultrasonidos también valoran el flujo sanguíneo." (Ultrasounds also measure blood flow.)

"suministrar" is a Spanish verb that means "to supply, provide". Example: "el objetivo es suministrar información sobre la situación financiera" (the objective is to provide information about the financial situation)
"islámico" is a Spanish adjective that means "Islamic". Example: "una guerra terrorista que el fundamentalista islámico ha lanzado" (a terrorist war that Islamic fundamentalist have launched)
"debilitar" is a Spanish verb that means "to weaken, debilitate". Example: "contribuyen a debilitar el sistema inmunológico" (they contribute to weakening the immune system)
"cristianismo" is a Spanish noun that means "Christianity". Example: "los creyentes en el cristianismo o catolicismo rezan el padre nuestro" (Christians or Catholics pray the Our Father)

"asignatura" is a Spanish noun that means "subject, course". Example: "los padres católicos eligen para sus hijos la educación católica" (Catholic parents choose a course in Catholic education for their children)
"pila" is a Spanish noun that means "(baptismal) font, battery, heap". Example: "Necesita más pilas para su reproductor de MP3." (He needs more batteries for his MP3 player.)
"ilustración" is a Spanish noun that means "illustration". Example: "libros de cuentos con hermosas ilustraciones" (storybooks with beautiful illustrations)
"exploración" is a Spanish noun that means "exploration". Example: "apoyaban la exploración de lugares remotos" (they supported the exploration of remote places)
"trayecto" is a Spanish noun that means "trajectory, course, path". Example: "¿Cuál ha sido su trayecto hasta llegar a director?" (What has been your path to becoming a director?)
"proveedor" is a Spanish noun that means "supplier, dealer". Example: "permitirá éxito a los proveedores de productos turísticos" (it will provide success to suppliers of tourist products)

"arquitectónico" is a Spanish adjective that means "architectural". Example: "edificios construidos de acuerdo con un moderno plan arquitectónico" (buildings that have been constructed with a modern architectural plan)
"litro" is a Spanish noun that means "liter". Example: "la botella nueva contiene 1 litro de leche" (the new bottle contains 1 liter of milk)
"pescador" is a Spanish noun that means "fisherman". Example: "muchos pescadores de la costa del Perú usan sus barcos tradicionales" (many fishermen on the Peruvian coast use traditional boats)
"himno" is a Spanish noun that means "hymn, anthem". Example: "entona el Himno Nacional con voz de barítono" (he sings the national anthem in a baritone voice)
"expandir" is a Spanish verb that means "to expand". Example: "el proyecto de expandir la capacidad" (the project of expanding the capacity)

"umbral" is a Spanish noun that means "threshold, outset". Example: "estuvo un momento en el umbral del empiezo de una nueva era" (he was there for a moment on the threshold of the beginning of a new age)
"regar" is a Spanish verb that means "to water, irrigate". Example: "mi madre usaba la manguera para regar el césped" (my mom would use the hose to water the grass)
"pulso" is a Spanish noun that means "pulse, pulsation". Example: "está nerviosa; su pulso se acelera" (she is nervous; her pulse is quickening)

s: "Competente" is a Spanish adjective describing someone with sufficient ability or skill. Example: "deben ser realizadas por una autoridad competente que sea imparcial" (they should be carried out by a competent authority that is unbiased)
q: What Spanish word is related to "able"?
e: "competente" is a Spanish adjective that means "competent, able". Example: "deben ser realizadas por una autoridad competente que sea imparcial" (they should be carried out by a competent authority that is unbiased)
"usado" is a Spanish adjective that means "worn out, old, used". Example: "Era arrugada como un pañuelo usado." (It was wrinkled like a used handkerchief.)

s: "Vitamina" is a Spanish noun referring to essential nutrients required for health. Example: "tiene el 100% de vitamina C para proteger tu sistema inmunológico" (it contains 100% of vitamin C to protect your immune system)
q: What Spanish word is related to "to forget"?
e: "vitamina" is a Spanish noun that means "vitamin". Example: "tiene el 100% de vitamina C para proteger tu sistema inmunológico" (it contains 100% of vitamin C to protect your immune system)
"laberinto" is a Spanish noun that means "maze, labyrinth". Example: "se perdió en el laberinto de callejones" (he got lost in a maze of alleys)
"persistir" is a Spanish verb that means "to persist". Example: "persiste esa historia, y es una buena historia" (that story persists, and it is a good story)

"excelencia" is a Spanish noun that means "excellence". Example: "para mí el conjunto por excelencia son los Beatles, me gustan muchísimo" (in my opinion, the band par excellence is the Beatles, I really like them)

"malestar" is a Spanish noun that means "discomfort, unease, unrest". Example: "sentí un inexplicable malestar y volví más cansada que otras veces" (I felt an unexplainable discomfort and I became more tired than at other times)
"grandeza" is a Spanish noun that means "greatness, size". Example: "vi la grandeza del equipo que gano el ultimo campeonato" (I saw the greatness of the team that won the last championship)
"descarga" is a Spanish noun that means "unloading, discharge". Example: "disponen de muelles para la carga y descarga de los buques" (docks for loading and unloading the ships are at their disposal)

"mixto" is a Spanish adjective that means "mixed, co-ed". Example: "estaban poblados por un grupo étnico mixto denominado siro-hitita" (they were populated by a mixed ethnic group, called Syro-Hittite)
"maleta" is a Spanish noun that means "suitcase, case". Example: "entra en ese momento con una maleta llena de ropa" (she enters at that moment with a suitcase full of clothes)
"alegar" is a Spanish verb that means "to plead, argue". Example: "lo privaron del derecho de alegar ante los Tribunales" (he was deprived of the right to plead before the courts)

s: filtrar means 'to seep, filter' in Spanish
e: Filtrar is a Spanish verb meaning to seep or filter, often used when light passes through an opening or when separating solids from liquids.
Traición is a noun meaning a severe breach of trust or loyalty, particularly in political contexts (treason) or personal relationships (betrayal).
